data.title: Ocugen: Buy Rating Reiterated as Analyst Highlights Retinal Pipeline Upside and Maintains $12 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Whitney Ijem from Canaccord Genuity maintained a Buy rating on Ocugen and keeping the price target at $12.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Whitney Ijem has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Ocugen’s positioning in large retinal disease markets with limited approved options and high unmet need. He highlights the potential of OCU410-ST in Stargardt disease, where no therapies are currently approved, and notes that emerging data and a differentiated mechanism could support meaningful clinical and commercial upside.

Whitney Ijem’s rating is based on the view that Ocugen is also leveraged to the evolving treatment landscape in geographic atrophy, a field with only a few existing products and significant room for better therapies. He maintains a $12 price target, unchanged, reflecting confidence that Ocugen’s pipeline, if successfully developed, can justify substantial value relative to the current market capitalization while investors gain exposure to multiple shots on goal in retinal indications.

In another report released on April 27, Noble Financial also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $12.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Insperity: Margin Progress Offsets Softer Volume as Outlook Cuts WSE Growth, Supporting Hold Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Andrew Nicholas has maintained their neutral stance on NSP stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Nicholas has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors relating to both growth and profitability. While Insperity exceeded expectations on gross profit and adjusted EBITDA thanks to disciplined cost control and slightly better benefit costs, earnings per share were constrained by a higher tax rate and the overall narrative remains one of stabilization rather than clear acceleration.

At the same time, management reduced its outlook for worksite employee growth, now anticipating a modest contraction driven by weaker new sales, elevated client attrition, and timing issues around net hiring, even as the mix shift and expense discipline allow the company to maintain its full-year EBITDA guidance. This balance of solid margin progress against softer volume trends and a more challenging demand backdrop leads Nicholas to see limited near-term upside in the shares, supporting a Hold rather than a more constructive rating.

In another report released today, Truist Financial also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $35.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Lifts Xenia Hotels & Resorts Price Target to $18 on Strong Momentum and Raised 2026 Guidance
data.description:
Ari Klein, an analyst from BMO Capital, maintained the Buy rating on Xenia Hotels & Resorts. The associated price target was raised to $18.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ari Klein has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Xenia Hotels & Resorts’ strong operating momentum and improved outlook. He highlights that first-quarter results outpaced expectations on key metrics such as RevPAR, EBITDA, and FFO, with particular strength coming from the Scottsdale property ramp and healthy out-of-room spending, even as food and beverage slightly lagged internal forecasts.

He also points to management’s decision to lift 2026 RevPAR and profit guidance as evidence of growing confidence in forward fundamentals, while noting that implied RevPAR growth for the remainder of the year still looks conservative in light of recent trends and a solid group booking pace. Additionally, he sees Xenia as well positioned to benefit from upcoming demand catalysts like the World Cup, and he reflects this constructive view by raising his price target from $17 to $18, reinforcing the favorable risk/reward profile underpinning his Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Klein is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 6.1% and a 57.26% success rate. Klein covers the Real Estate sector, focusing on stocks such as Equinix, Digital Realty, and SBA Communications.

data.title: Atlassian’s Renewed Cloud Momentum and AI Monetization Upside Support Reiterated Buy Rating and Unchanged $150 Price Target
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity analyst David Hynes has maintained their bullish stance on TEAM stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Hynes has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Atlassian’s renewed growth and emerging AI monetization. He points to a clear inflection in cloud performance, with faster revenue growth, improving net retention, and strong early traction from AI features like Rovo, where users are expanding their spend roughly twice as fast as non‑users, supported by the scalable Teamwork Graph and increasing MCP activity.

He also highlights Collections as a powerful revenue driver, noting robust adoption in both Teamwork and Service offerings, broadening use cases beyond IT, and measurable operational benefits for customers from AI‑driven automation. Combined with a solid backlog, durable mid‑20% cloud growth, nearly 30% free cash flow margins, and a valuation of roughly 3x EV/revenue for a business capable of 20% growth at scale, he views the current price as attractive and thus reiterates a $150 target and Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, KeyBanc also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $130.00 price target.

data.title: Rivian Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Analyst Sees Scaling Momentum, Strong Liquidity and Confirms $22 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst George Gianarikas of Canaccord Genuity maintained a Buy rating on Rivian Automotive, retaining the price target of $22.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

George Gianarikas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Rivian’s scaling strategy, product strength, and funding visibility. He views the company’s decision to expand initial capacity at the Georgia plant to 300,000 mid-sized vehicles annually as a clear signal that Rivian is preparing for meaningful volume, cost efficiencies, and long-term growth, supported by a restructured DOE loan and an unchanged $22 price target.

He also highlights Rivian’s sizable $14 billion liquidity position, reinforced by expected contributions from partners like Volkswagen and Uber, as a key buffer on the path to profitability. Combined with strong reception for the R1 and the disruptive potential of the R2 in a U.S. market lacking a clear non-Tesla EV leader, he believes Rivian is well placed to emerge as the leading mass-market challenger as EV adoption and consumer interest improve.

In another report released today, Benchmark Co. also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $25.00 price target.

RIVN’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $12.990 to $16.400, which is a 26.25% increase.
